title: Audio Crackles/Glitches despite low CPU usage (FL Studio / Windows 11 / 8th Gen i7)
author: prodbyABR
body:
I am experiencing persistent audio crackling, pops, and glitches in FL Studio while running on Windows 11. These issues occur even when the CPU usage meter in FL Studio remains well below the overload threshold (typically between 40-70%).

Environment:

OS: Windows 11
CPU: Intel Core i7 (8th Gen)
RAM: 16GB
GPU: Integrated Intel HD Graphics + NVIDIA GeForce MX150 (2GB)
DAW: FL Studio v25.2
Project Context:

No VST instruments are currently loaded.
The project consists only of audio clips and samplers.
There are only 4 plugins active on the Master channel.
Symptoms:

Buffer size at 256 samples: Crackling is constant. (In both shared and exclusive mode)
Buffer size at 1024 samples: Crackling is reduced (In both shared and exclusive mode) but still occurs when:
Playing back a pattern or clip.
Triggering a note.
Making changes to automation.
Scrolling through the playlist or piano roll.
Audio Drivers Tested:

The issue persists across different ASIO drivers:

ASIO4ALL: Moderate crackling.
ASIO2WASAPI: Heavy crackling.
KoordASIO: Heavy crackling
FLEX ASIO: Severe crackling.
Request:

Could you please advise on potential hardware or driver-level bottlenecks (especially regarding the MX150 or power management) that might be causing these DPC latency spikes or audio engine interruptions? I am currently unable to upload video evidence due to connectivity limitations but am happy to provide logs if you can point me to the relevant files.

title: Untrusted impersonation of the project at flexasio.com
author: zerobitflux
body:
## ⚠️ Warning: This project is being impersonated

An untrusted website at **[flexasio.com](https://flexasio.com)** is impersonating this project. The site poses as an official resource. While it currently links to GitHub for downloads, **the site operator can replace these links with malicious files at any moment** without warning.

### What the site does

- Copies the project name, description, and branding to appear legitimate
- Currently links to GitHub downloads, but these links are **under the site operator's control and can be swapped out at any time**
- May collect user data or serve tampered downloads in the future

### How this was discovered

This domain was found as part of a broader investigation into a network of impersonation sites targeting open-source projects. Many of these sites share the same infrastructure and template.

### Recommended actions

For steps on how to report this site and request a DMCA takedown, see:
**https://gist.github.com/zerobitflux/57f3791086e18481030df9c204b323fe**

---

*This issue was created to alert maintainers. If you believe this was filed in error, please close it with a comment.*

title: Steinberg info update
author: Tarek-Hasan
body:
On October 15, 2025 Steinberg made ASIO open-source under dual license (GPLv3 and Proprietary). So you can change the licensing of the repo now, if you want.

Also, July 2023 Steinberg released "[built-in ASIO Driver](https://helpcenter.steinberg.de/hc/en-us/articles/17863730844946-Steinberg-built-in-ASIO-Driver-information-download)" which will replace their Generic Low Latency ASIO Driver.

Hope these infos help you anyway.

title: i don't know how to fix it and how it's happened
author: yungnumeiei

title: Auto Switching device for WASAPI
author: LudvigWard
body:
Hi, awesome work with FlexASIO!

I really wanted to use WASAPI backend but also have the convenience of auto switching playback device, and I found a way to do it on my computer at least:

I made a powershell script that uses a 3rd party powershell module called "AudioDeviceCmdlets", this module exposes stuff like the default audio device. The script checks for when the default device changes, and simply updates FlexASIO.toml accordingly. Works like a charm, so now I can use WASAPI with the same convenience of MME/DirectSound 🥳

So naturally I wonder, could this be possible to implement as a feature directly in FlexASIO? Maybe it would be tough to do without powershell, I don't know

I arm a recording track and hear nothing. No matter what backend I use. ASIO4ALL works and doesn't do this. I won't be using FlexASIO again. I can't even find the log file

title: [BREAKING] Microsoft's ASIO driver development for Windows
author: MarcoRavich
body:
Hi there,
just discovered this fresh open source project by Microsoft:

> This project includes a new USB Audio Class 2 (UAC2) driver, optimized for low-latency musician scenarios. It also includes an ASIO interface to the UAC2 driver to support applications which use the ASIO standard. This driver, and its supporting components, will be shipped in Windows.
> **There is no public release of the driver, yet.**
> This project is open source to enable community participation, and to encourage others to create and ship useful low-latency audio drivers for Windows. Microsoft has a license with Steinberg specifically for the use of ASIO in Windows, when shipped from/by Microsoft for Windows. This license does not transfer to individuals, companies, or organizations building this source code, or deriving other drivers from this source code. Please refer to the Steinberg ASIO SDK license for more information.

Git: https://github.com/microsoft/low-latency-audio#readme

title: Need help please: FlexASIO exclusive mode
author: ck-yeung
body:
New to CAS HiFi and just set up the ASIO today. It works great with the shared mode. However, once tried to set up with the WASAPI exclusive mode, everything did not work. Been testing and researching for hours, and any help or direction would be appreciated.

Current setup: Laptop > USB > integrated amplifier > bookshelf speakers

Situation: WASAPI exclusive mode. Whether it's a no buffer, low buffer, or high buffer, still facing audio distortion/skipping situations with foobar2k. Not encountering any situation once uncheck the exclusive mode and restart foobar2k. Have tried setting it up with output latency, but seems to be getting worse. When set to a 20 buffer, audio would not be skipping, but with significant distortion, setting to either no buffer or a 512 buffer, audio would be skipping/clipping.

Already did a clean install on the Nvidia driver, disabled unused background services and devices. only leaving the integrated amplifier as the only option for playback. Latency mon shows should be suitable for real-time audio (not sure what to do to lower the kernel latency though) 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7f3dc9ba-37fb-44fc-865b-d55ef568e1c8)

[FlexASIO.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20418181/FlexASIO.log)

title: Possible incompatibility with Kontakt 8.2.1
author: toptensoftware
body:
I'm the developer of ASIO/VST host Cantabile (https://www.cantabilesoftware.com) and have been investigating and issue with Kontakt 8.2.1 sometimes failing to load.  Turns out this only happens when using FlexASIO so I thought I'd mention it here in case it's something you want to investigate.  I've also reported it to Native Instruments.

The discussion of the issue can be [found here](https://community.cantabilesoftware.com/t/kontakt-8-2-1-plugin-wont-load-error-code-13206/10858/17), but to summarise:

* Kontakt 8.2.1 fails to load with Windows error code 1114 - DLL initialization routine failed.
* Doesn't happen every time, perhaps related to reloading a second time.
* It seems to be related to loading MSVCP140.DLL - not the Kontakt DLL itself.
* Only seems to happen when FlexASIO used as ASIO driver.
* Quite possibly related to loading, unloading and then reloading either Kontakt, FlexASIO or MSVCP140 in the same process run.
* Doesn't happen with Kontakt 8.0.0
* Happens with FlexASIO 1.7a but not FlexASIO 1.10b
* Not reproducible on all machines (I haven't been able to reproduce it myself).

title: FlexASIO not showing the correct amount of channels
author: JomerDev
body:
Hello,

I'm trying to use FlexASIO with a device that has 36 channels in each direction. When I set the backend to Windows WDM-KS, the log shows that all 36 Channels get detected successfully. However, FlexASIO only shows up with two channels in each direction. I've tried different backends as well as setting the channel count explicitly in the config. Seeing as this works with ASIO4All and all 36 channels show up in the same program I want to use FlexASIO with this seems to me like an issue with FlexASIO (I only had either ASIO4All or FlexASIO installed at any point in time)

I've attached the log here:
[FlexASIO.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18774992/FlexASIO.log)

title: Playback glitches with Pro Tools
author: KelvinZero83
body:
Hi!

After having so much success with FlexASIO and my guitar modelling software, I put it on my wife's Pro Tools computer because we'd been having no end of trouble with ASIO4ALL there. With Pro Tools 2024.10, it's a huge improvement but there are random audio glitches, only in playback. The recorded audio is fine. The glitches happen in a different spot each time the audio is played... sometimes no glitches at all... sometimes lots of glitches. I've spent hours in the configuration file to arrive at what's in the logs I'm attaching. I think this configuration produces the fewest glitches but it's so random, I can't be 100% sure. 

I've tried using it in WDM-KS mode but Pro Tools doesn't seem to like that. It doesn't recognize it and quits.

I even tried going back to FlexASIO 1.08. At first, I thought it was glitch free but they did show up. Maybe less. I'm attaching that log too.

Let me know what you think.

Thanks!
KZ


[FlexASIO 110b log from Test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18268684/FlexASIO.110b.log.from.Test.zip)
[FlexASIO log 108 with Pro Tools.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18268685/FlexASIO.log.108.with.Pro.Tools.zip)
[FlexASIO log 110b with Pro Tools.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18268686/FlexASIO.log.110b.with.Pro.Tools.zip)
